title: Use logging instead of print()
guideline: Application code must not call print(); use the module-level logger instead.

rule.message: print() is forbidden; use logger.info/debug instead

good_example:
import logging

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

def greet(name):
    logger.info("hello %s", name)

title: Function names are snake_case
guideline: All function and method names use snake_case (dunder names allowed).

title: No mutable default arguments
guideline: Never use list/dict literals as default parameter values; default to None and initialize inside the function.

rule.message: mutable default argument; use None and initialize inside the body

title: Functions carry docstrings
guideline: Every function definition starts with a docstring describing behavior.
bad_example:
def add(a, b):
    return a + b

title: No eval()/exec()
guideline: Dynamic code execution via eval/exec is forbidden; use explicit dispatch instead.

rule.message: eval()/exec() is forbidden; use explicit dispatch

good_example:
handlers = {"add": lambda a, b: a + b}
result = handlers["add"](1, 2)

title: No bare except
guideline: Always catch a specific exception type; a bare `except:` swallows KeyboardInterrupt and SystemExit.

rule.message: bare except is forbidden; catch a specific exception type

title: pytest test function shape
guideline: Tests are plain functions with arrange/act/assert, using fixtures as parameters.
good_example:
def test_load_user_returns_profile(store):
    """Loading an existing user yields its profile."""
    store.add(make_user(id=7))
    profile = load_user(store, 7)
    assert profile.id == 7

title: dataclass model shape
guideline: Plain data carriers are frozen dataclasses with typed fields and a to_dict helper.

title: FastAPI route handler shape
guideline: Route handlers are async, typed, and return response models directly.
